slides.title: The quiet upside of *citrus*
slides.sub: Oranges and their cousins carry plant compounds linked to healthy blood flow. Here is why they matter as you age.

slides.title: Flavonoids in every segment
slides.body: Citrus fruits are rich in *flavonoids*, natural plant compounds. One of the best studied is hesperidin, concentrated in oranges and their peel.

slides.title: Support for your vessels
slides.body: Hesperidin *may support* the inner lining of blood vessels, the thin layer that helps them relax, flex and carry blood smoothly.

slides.title: Steadier circulation
slides.body: Healthy, flexible vessels are *linked to* better circulation. That is part of how the body keeps heart and brain well supplied over time.

slides.title: Vessels stiffen with time
slides.body: Blood vessels naturally lose some flexibility with age. Habits that *support vessel function* are a quiet investment in later decades.

slides.title: Eat the whole fruit
slides.body: Whole oranges, grapefruit and mandarins beat juice. The *fiber and pith* carry much of the flavonoid benefit, with less sugar.

slides.title: Build the habit, *see it stick*.
slides.body: Vibe Health logs the food, movement, steps and sleep behind your circulation, then folds it into one daily Health Score you can actually follow.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
A halved orange is more than a snack. Its flavonoids, like hesperidin, are linked to healthy blood vessels and steady circulation. 🍊🌿

Eat the whole fruit, not just the juice, so you keep the fiber and pith where much of the benefit lives. Save this for your next grocery run. 🔖

Vibe Health turns everyday choices like this into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Citrus Fruits May Support Healthy Circulation as You Age
